ddls:
CREATE TABLE "claims" (
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claim */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer making the claim */,
  "incident_cause_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident cause */,
  "claim_date" TEXT /* Date the claim was made */,
  "claim_area_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim area */,
  "police_report" TEXT /* Whether a police report was filed */,
  "claim_type" TEXT /* Type of claim (e.g., Material only) */,
  "claim_amount" REAL /* Amount of the claim */,
  "total_policy_claims" INTEGER /* Total number of claims made by the policyholder */,
  "fraudulent" INTEGER /* Whether the claim is suspected to be fraudulent */,
  "claim_status" TEXT /* Current status of the claim (e.g., Open, In Progress, Closed) */,
  "adjuster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assigned claims adjuster */,
  "underwriter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assigned underwriter */,
  "loss_date" TEXT /* Date of the incident leading to the claim */,
  "claim_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the claim */,
  "total_claim_amount" REAL /* Total amount claimed */,
  "approved_claim_amount" REAL /* Amount approved for payment */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date the claim was paid */,
  "claim_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments related to the claim */,
  "fraud_score" REAL /* Numerical score indicating the likelihood of fraud */,
  "fraud_reason" TEXT /* Description of why the claim is suspected to be fraudulent */,
  "policy_number" TEXT /* Unique number of the policy associated with the claim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("claim_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "adjusters" (
  "adjuster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each adjuster */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the adjuster */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the adjuster */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the adjuster */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the adjuster */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the adjuster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("adjuster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "underwriters" (
  "underwriter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each underwriter */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the underwriter */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the underwriter */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the underwriter */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the underwriter */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the underwriter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("underwriter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claim area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the claim area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident cause */,
  "incident_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident cause */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "claim_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* ID of the claim */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the claim */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the status change */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes related to the status change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fraud_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fraud rule */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the fraud rule */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the fraud rule */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used in the fraud rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id")
)
column_names: ID of the customer making the claim
